title: Pelican to Murder — Level 2 Rookie Detective
description: Level 2 version with clear but connected venue, money, and digital evidence.

answerKey.method: Switched Lena’s allergy-safe drink and hid her injector
answerKey.motive: To stop Lena from exposing the stolen sponsor money
answerKey.keyEvidence: Camera export gap, safe access log, and fake sponsor invoices
answerKey.explanation: Owen had the financial motive, the office access, and the ability to alter both the drink path and the camera export. The safe log and fake invoices point to the sponsor theft, while the camera gap proves he tried to hide the moment that connects him to the switch.

publicClues.title: Case Opened
publicClues.text: Pelican to Murder begins as a live investigation. This session is Level 2: Rookie Detective Case.

publicClues.title: Difficulty Loaded
publicClues.text: This is Level 2: Rookie Detective Case. Evidence and answers are unique to this level.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 1 Available
publicClues.text: Round 1 is ready. Answer the checkpoint after reviewing the collapse.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 2 Available
publicClues.text: Round 2 is ready. Compare timeline movement before answering.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 3 Available
publicClues.text: Round 3 is ready. Digital evidence now matters.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 4 Available
publicClues.text: Round 4 is ready. Find the motive evidence.

publicClues.title: Final Accusation Open
publicClues.text: Final questions are open. Complete all 10 total questions before the lock.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Lena missed two calls from Owen at 7:41 and 7:44 PM.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: A third call from Owen lasted nine seconds as she left the host table.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Lena texted Owen: “We need to talk before the reveal.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Owen replied: “Not here. Patio corridor in two.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Lena’s phone moved to the patio corridor at 8:07 PM.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Owen’s phone appeared in the same corridor for under one minute.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A sponsor deposit appears once in the public ledger and once under a private vendor label.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The private vendor payout routes to a prepaid account tied to Owen’s office computer.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A guest photo shows Owen near the bar service rail during Tessa’s distraction.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Another photo shows Lena holding a glass without her normal allergy marker.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: Brielle’s livestream catches Owen stepping behind the bar before the clip cuts.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The posted version removes the seconds before Lena receives her drink.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Owen saved “Mars Event Services,” which traces to a prepaid sponsor payment line.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: Lena saved the same contact with the note “fake vendor?”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: the killer needed both venue access and a reason to silence Lena.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the drink switch and missing injector point to planning, not panic.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: Security export skips the exact patio corridor window.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: The file properties show the export was made from Owen’s manager login.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: Owen searched how to remove metadata from security clips.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: His browser history also includes vendor invoice templates.

rounds.dialogue: Round 1 is open. Focus on the collapse. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 1 is nearly complete. Lock in what the collapse proves before the next round opens.

rounds.title: Round 4: The Sponsor Money
rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the sponsor money and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 4 is open. Focus on the sponsor money. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 4 is nearly complete. Lock in what the sponsor money proves before the next round opens.

rounds.title: Round 5: The Office Cover-Up
rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the office cover-up and answer the checkpoint question.

rounds.title: Final Accusation
rounds.objective: Answer the final five questions to close the case.
rounds.dialogue: The final accusation window is open. Choose the culprit, method, motive, key evidence, and the statement that fails.
rounds.countdownReview: The final window is closing. Submit all answers before the case locks.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Pelican to Murder. You are playing Level 2: Rookie Detective Case. Study each app carefully. Every level has its own truth path, evidence trail, and final answer set.
revealScript: Case closed. This was Level 2: Rookie Detective Case. The culprit was Owen Landry. The method: Switched Lena’s allergy-safe drink and hid her injector. The motive: To stop Lena from exposing the stolen sponsor money. The proof: Camera export gap, safe access log, and fake sponsor invoices.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: What detail makes Lena’s collapse suspicious?

options.label: Her injector is missing after the wrong drink appears
A napkin is folded the wrong way

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 2: Which timeline issue matters most?

options.label: Owen and Lena overlap in the corridor window

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 3: Which digital clue suggests evidence was altered?

options.label: The security export gap from Owen’s login

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 4: What financial clue points to motive?

options.label: Fake sponsor invoices tied to Owen

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 5: Which paired clues connect the cover-up?

options.label: A lost wristband at the bar
Manager safe access plus camera export login

questions.prompt: Final 2: How was the murder carried out?

options.label: Switched the allergy-safe drink and hid the injector
Staged a fall near the patio
To hide the stolen sponsor money

questions.prompt: Final 4: Which evidence best proves it?

options.label: Camera gap, safe log, and fake invoices

questions.prompt: Final 5: Which suspect statement falls apart?

options.label: Owen said he never left the office
Tessa said she marked Lena’s glass
Brielle said she filmed the toast
Cole said he stayed by sound
